response.justification: While Szabo brings strong technical depth in distributed systems and backend architecture with 19+ years of experience, he lacks critical qualifications for this executive CTO role. There is no evidence of team leadership, scaling engineering organizations, or managing external delivery partners — essential for the position. His fintech experience is minimal with no exposure to credit systems, payment infrastructure, or regulated financial environments. Though he recently gained exposure to agentic AI systems at re:cinq, insufficient detail exists on production-scale complexity, failure modes, or LLM evaluation methodology required for this role's AI-native fintech focus.
strengths: Deep backend and distributed-systems expertise with proven experience building microservices handling millions of requests per second and event-driven architectures
19+ years of consistent engineering growth with multiple senior and staff-level positions across diverse technical domains demonstrating breadth and adaptability
Strong commitment to software craftsmanship, testing discipline (TDD), and knowledge-sharing evidenced through formal teaching and mentoring activities
concerns: No demonstrated experience in team leadership, hiring, or scaling engineering organizations from 10–30+ people — a core CTO responsibility explicitly required
Minimal fintech infrastructure background with no visible experience in credit systems, payment flows, working-capital management, or regulated financial environments
Multi-agent AI systems experience is recent (13 months) with insufficient detail on production-scale complexity, failure handling, observability, or LLM evaluation methodology
